question: What AI startups raised Series A funding in the last 48 hours?
answer: Based on the latest funding news from April 23–24, 2026, three AI startups raised notable Series A rounds: Aaru ($80M, synthetic AI research), Runware ($50M, generative AI inference), and Ankar ($20M, AI-powered patent lifecycle). Additionally, Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is in talks for its first-ever funding round, with Tencent and Alibaba reported as potential participants.

key_findings.claim: Aaru raised an $80M Series A for its platform that uses simulated AI agents to predict consumer and political behavior, bringing total equity funding to $88M.
Runware raised a $50M Series A to scale its 'Sonic Inference Engine,' which optimizes generative AI performance via a single API aggregating hundreds of thousands of models. Total raised is now $66M.
Ankar, founded by Palantir veterans, raised a $20M Series A to modernize the patent lifecycle using AI, analyzing over 150M patent applications and scientific papers. Total capital is now $24M.
Tencent and Alibaba are in talks to participate in the first-ever funding round for Chinese AI startup DeepSeek, per a Bloomberg report published April 24, 2026.

sources.title: Top Startup and Tech Funding News – April 23, 2026

limitations: Real-time funding databases (e.g., Crunchbase, PitchBook) may have additional Series A deals not yet publicly announced or indexed.
The DeepSeek round has not yet been formally confirmed or assigned a Series letter.
Some deals may have been announced but not yet covered by accessible news sources within the 48-hour window.
assumptions: 'Last 48 hours' is interpreted as April 23–24, 2026, based on today's date.
Only publicly reported and confirmed Series A rounds are included.
next_steps: Monitor Crunchbase Daily or PitchBook for any additional Series A deals announced on April 24, 2026.
Track the DeepSeek funding round for confirmation of round stage and size.
Check TechCrunch and Bloomberg for any late-breaking Series A announcements.

judge_verdict.reasoning: All listed claims are directly supported by the provided source snippets, including the DeepSeek funding talks.
